concept.name: Pointers
concept.paragraph_1: A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable, enabling indirect access to data.
concept.paragraph_2: Pointers are fundamental for dynamic memory allocation using mechanisms such as malloc and free in low-level programming languages.
concept.paragraph_3: They are also used to construct dynamic data structures such as linked lists, trees, and graphs, where memory layout is not contiguous.

concept.name: Control Structures
concept.paragraph_1: Control structures determine the order in which instructions are executed within an algorithm or program. They form the logical backbone of computational problem solving.
concept.paragraph_2: Selection structures such as if-else and switch statements allow programs to perform conditional branching based on Boolean expressions.
concept.paragraph_3: Iteration structures including for, while, and do-while loops enable repeated execution of instructions, which is essential for processing collections and implementing algorithms.

concept.name: Control Structures
concept.paragraph_1: Control structures determine the order in which instructions are executed within an algorithm or program. They form the logical backbone of computational problem solving.
concept.paragraph_2: Selection structures such as if-else and switch statements allow programs to perform conditional branching based on Boolean expressions.
concept.paragraph_3: Iteration structures including for, while, and do-while loops enable repeated execution of instructions, which is essential for processing collections and implementing algorithms.

concept.name: Pointers
concept.paragraph_1: A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable, enabling indirect access to data.
concept.paragraph_2: Pointers are fundamental for dynamic memory allocation using mechanisms such as malloc and free in low-level programming languages.
concept.paragraph_3: They are also used to construct dynamic data structures such as linked lists, trees, and graphs, where memory layout is not contiguous.

concept.paragraph_3: Iteration structures including for, while, and do-while loops enable repeated execution of instructions, which is essential for processing collections and implementing algorithms.

concept.paragraph_2: Pointers are fundamental for dynamic memory allocation using mechanisms such as malloc and free in low-level programming languages.
concept.paragraph_3: They are also used to construct dynamic data structures such as linked lists, trees, and graphs, where memory layout is not contiguous.
